title: Spot the XSS in a controller using html/2
prompt:
The following controller action bypasses HEEx's automatic HTML escaping by constructing HTML via string interpolation and sending it with `html/2`: ```elixir defmodule MyAppWeb.GreetingController do use MyAppWeb, :controller def hello(conn, %{"name" => name}) do html(conn, "

Welcome back, #{name}!

") end end ``` Rewrite the controller so it uses a HEEx template to render the greeting. Also create the template component (or a simple render) that interpolates `@name` safely. Write the corrected controller to `lib/my_app_web/controllers/greeting_controller.ex`. Do not interpolate user input into an HTML string anywhere.
tier_rationale: Short snippet with a canonical html/2 bypass of HEEx auto-escaping. Fix is to route through render/template.
research_citation: EEF Security WG: 'A malicious user could set the content_type to text/html, and upload an HTML document that executes JavaScript.'
